- `src/trading.py` — P&L aggregation (`calculate_pnl`, `apply_commissions`, `realized_vs_unrealized`)

None of these implement order matching (no order book, no threading, no locks, no matching logic). The closest file by name, `src/orders.py`, is only ~31 lines of dataclass + a simple cancel helper — there's no concurrency in it, so a race condition isn't really possible there either (though `cancel_order` mutates `order.status` without a lock, that's only a concern if *something else* were concurrently matching, which doesn't exist here).
